entries.definition:
ẓ-n-n assumption, supposition, conjecture, guessing; opinion, belief, to realise, to know; doubt, to have a low opinion of, to think ill of, to be suspicious, to accuse. Contexts in which derivatives of this root occur vary greatly as regards the degree of doubt/certainty associated with them, and therefore cause interpretational problems. Of this root, four forms occur 69 times in the Qurʾan: ظَنَّ ẓanna 47 times; ظَنّ ẓann 20 times; ظُنُون ẓunūn once and ظَانِّين ẓānnīn once.
ظَنَّ ẓanna u the senior sister of a group of verbs which, functionally, may take two direct objects and semantically, imply lack of certainty of the predication in varying degrees (ظنّ وأخواتها). According to ʾal-Rāghib the use of أَنَّ ʾanna in the predication of ظنّ indicates a high position on the certainty-doubtfulness scale, whereas low position is indicated by the use of أَنْ ʾan. An example of the former is (75:28) وَظَنَّ أَنَّهُ الْفِرَاقُ and he [the dying man] realised it is the final parting, and an example of the latter is (48:12) بَلْ ظَنَنتُمْ أَنْ لَنْ يَنْقَلِبَ الرَّسُولُ وَالْمُؤْمِنُونَ إِلَى أَهْلِيهِمْ أَبَدًا but you thought/hoped that the Messenger and the believers would never return to their families. In context, various meanings are associated with ظنّ, among them the following 1 to know, to come to realise (38:24) وَظَنَّ دَاوُودُ أَنَّمَا فَتَنَّاهُ and David came to realise that We had put him to the test 2 to deem (17:102) وَإِنِّي لَأَظُنُّكَ يَا فِرْعَوْنُ مَثْبُورًا and indeed, I deem you, Pharaoh, ruined/vanquished 3 to assume, to surmise, to imagine (21:87) وَذَا النُّونِ إِذ ذَّهَبَ مُغَاضِبًا فَظَنَّ أَن لَّن نَّقْدِرَ عَلَيْهِ and

589

589
